chapter_context.summary: Jezebel threatens Elijah's life, driving him to flee to Horeb in despair; God speaks to him in a 'still small voice' and commissions him to anoint Hazael, Jehu, and Elisha as his successor.

outline.text: Jezebel Threatens Elijah; He Flees to Beersheba and Horeb
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jezebel Threatens Elijah; He Flees to Beersheba and Horeb (19:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah at Horeb; Wind, Earthquake, Fire, and the Still Small Voice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah at Horeb; Wind, Earthquake, Fire, and the Still Small Voice (19:9-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: God's Commission: Anoint Hazael, Jehu, and Elisha; The 7000 Remnant
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Commission: Anoint Hazael, Jehu, and Elisha; The 7000 Remnant (19:15-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Calls Elisha; Elisha's Farewell Feast
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Calls Elisha; Elisha's Farewell Feast (19:19-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 19, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
